title: Juergen Schmidhuber | AI Glossary
excerpts:
Section Title: Juergen Schmidhuber > Definition
Content:
Pioneer in artificial neural networks who developed long short-term memory (LSTM) networks and universal AI concepts.
Section Title: Juergen Schmidhuber > Detailed Explanation
Content:
Juergen Schmidhuber’s work includes the development of LSTM networks, which revolutionized sequence learning in AI. He has made significant contributions to meta-learning, hierarchical reinforcement learning, and artificial curiosity. His research on universal AI and optimal ordered problem solver algorithms has influenced modern deep learning approaches.

||The New York Times headlined: "When A.I. Matures, It May Call Jürgen Schmidhuber 'Dad'."
Since age 15 or so, his main goal has been to build a self-improving Artificial Intelligence (AI) smarter than himself, then retire. His lab's Deep Learning Neural Networks (NNs)
based on ideas published in the "Annus Mirabilis" 1990-1991 have revolutionised machine learning and AI. In 2009, the [CTC-trained](https://sferics.idsia.ch/pub/juergen/icml2006.pdf) Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) of his team was the first recurrent NN to win international pattern recognition competitions . In 2010, his lab's fast and deep feedforward NNs on GPUs greatly outperformed previous methods , without using any unsupervised pre-training, a popular deep learning strategy that he pioneered in 1991 .

title: AI Speaker Jürgen Schmidhuber
excerpts:
Section Title: Jürgen Schmidhuber > Jürgen Schmidhuber biography
Content:
AI speaker Jürgen Schmidhuber is a computer scientist who is well-known for his deep learning, artificial intelligence, and artificial neural networks work.
The primary ambition of professor Jürgen Schmidhuber has been to develop a self-improving Artificial Intelligence that is smarter than him. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and other Deep Learning Neural Networks developed by his lab have transformed AI and machine learning.
By 2017, these deep learning neural networks were on 3 billion devices. Even famous companies like Google, Apple, and Facebook started utilizing them. This resulted in improved speech recognition on over 2 billion Android phones, and improved translation with Google Translate. Moreover, several other applications including Amazon Alexa started using these deep learning neural networks.

title: JÜRGEN SCHMIDHUBER - Professor, Computer Science
excerpts:
Section Title: JÜRGEN SCHMIDHUBER > ```
Content:
Prior to joining KAUST, he worked as Scientific Director of the Swiss AI Lab, IDSIA, and as professor of Artificial Intelligence (Ordinarius) at the University of Lugano (USI) from 2009-2021. He is most noted for his pioneering work in the field of artificial intelligence (AI), deep learning, and artificial neural networks. In 1990-91, he laid foundations of GenAI by introducing the principles of Generative Adversarial Networks (now used for deepfakes), unnormalised linear Transformers (see the T in ChatGPT), and self-supervised Pre-Training for deep learning (see the P in ChatGPT). His lab also produced LSTM, the most cited AI of the 20th century, and the Highway Net (a variant of which is the most cited AI of the 21st century). Elon Musk tweeted: "Schmidhuber invented everything." His AI is on over 3 billion smartphones, and used many billions of times per day.

title: Jürgen Schmidhuber - Wikipedia
excerpts:
Section Title: Career > Research
Content:
Schmidhuber supervised the 1991 diploma thesis of his student Sepp Hochreiter <sup>[[ 18 ]]()</sup> which he considered "one of the most important documents in the history of machine learning". <sup>[[ 15 ]]()</sup> It studied the *neural history compressor* , <sup>[[ 14 ]]()</sup> and more importantly analyzed and overcame the vanishing gradient problem . This led to the long short-term memory (LSTM), a type of recurrent neural network . The name LSTM was introduced in a tech report (1995) leading to the most cited LSTM publication (1997), co-authored by Hochreiter and Schmidhuber. <sup>[[ 19 ]]()</sup> It was not yet the standard LSTM architecture which is used in almost all current applications. The standard LSTM architecture was introduced in 2000 by Felix Gers , Schmidhuber, and Fred Cummins.
